reasoning: There have been exactly two rate cuts in 2025 so far (a 25 bps cut in September and another 25 bps cut in October). To reach exactly four rate cuts for the year by the December 10, 2025 meeting, the Fed would need to issue a 50 bps cut in December (which counts as two 25 bps cuts under the resolution criteria). A 50 bps cut at a single meeting is historically unusual without an explicit economic emergency. Market consensus heavily anticipates a standard 25 bps cut for December (which would leave the annual total at three cuts). The Polymarket price was sitting at 2% after the October cut, which accurately reflects the very slim chance of a sudden shift to a 50 bps cut. Therefore, my probability aligns with the market's 2% estimate.
